title: Paired Pisano Divisibility — Fibonacci pairs have 2.25x higher both-divide rate across 499 moduli

mechanism.lcm_principle: For coprime (p,q), both p|pi(m) AND q|pi(m) iff lcm(p,q)=p*q divides pi(m). Fibonacci pairs have smaller products, so their lcm divides pi(m) more often.
mechanism.individual_advantage: Fibonacci numbers individually divide pi(m) at 3.70x the rate of non-Fibonacci numbers (from prior [163] analysis). The paired advantage compounds both effects.
mechanism.exception: 4:1 has higher rate than some Fibonacci pairs at order-3 because lcm(4,1)=4 is very small. This explains Kirkwood gap 4:1 being as deep as Fibonacci resonances.

qa_connection.pisano_period: pi(m) = period of Fibonacci sequence mod m = orbit period of T-operator [[0,1],[1,1]] in Z/mZ
qa_connection.divisibility_identity: F_k | F_{nk} (every k-th Fibonacci divides every (nk)-th). Fibonacci numbers divide pi(m) when the T-operator orbit aligns with the Fibonacci index.
qa_connection.paired_resonance: A p:q resonance is 'deep' when BOTH p and q divide pi(m), meaning the T-operator orbit has commensurability with both components simultaneously.
qa_connection.kirkwood_connection: Kirkwood gap 4:1 exception: lcm(4,1)=4 is smaller than many Fibonacci products, so 4:1 paired divisibility is high despite 4 not being Fibonacci.

axiom_compliance.A1: Fibonacci indices and moduli in {1,...,500}, positive integers only
axiom_compliance.A2: Pisano period derived from modular Fibonacci recurrence: F_{k+1} = (F_k + F_{k-1}) mod m
axiom_compliance.T2: p-values, Mann-Whitney U, and both-divide rates are observer projections of discrete divisibility
axiom_compliance.S1: m*m used instead of m**2 in Pisano computation (line 35 of source)
axiom_compliance.S2: All QA state (Fibonacci indices, moduli, Pisano periods) is integer

caveats: Product-matched control shows only 56% Fibonacci wins (5/9) — the advantage is partially explained by smaller products.
4:1 exception shows non-Fibonacci pairs can achieve high both-divide rates when lcm is small.
Modulus range 2..500 is finite; asymptotic behavior may differ.
Order-1 comparison has small Fibonacci sample (2 pairs vs 7 non-Fibonacci), limiting statistical power.
The 2.25x overall advantage conflates multiple orders; per-order advantages range from 1.77x to 3.12x.
sources: qa_pisano_paired_divisibility.py — Will Dale conjecture, Claude computation (2026-04-02)
Family [163] QA_FIBONACCI_RESONANCE_CERT.v1 — individual Fibonacci divisibility of pi(m)
Wall (1960) — Fibonacci entry point and Pisano period properties
Renault (1996) — The Fibonacci Sequence Under Various Moduli
